title: MCPServer
description: MCPServer is the Schema for the mcpservers API

spec.description: MCPServerSpec defines the desired state of MCPServer

args.description: Args are additional arguments to pass to the MCP server

audit.description: Audit defines audit logging configuration for the MCP server

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether audit logging is enabled
When true, enables audit logging with default configuration

authServerRef.description:
AuthServerRef optionally references a resource that configures an embedded
OAuth 2.0/OIDC authorization server to authenticate MCP clients.
Currently the only supported kind is MCPExternalAuthConfig (type: embeddedAuthServer).

kind.description: Kind identifies the type of the referenced resource.

name.description: Name is the name of the referenced resource in the same namespace.

authzConfig.description: AuthzConfig defines authorization policy configuration for the MCP server

configMap.description:
ConfigMap references a ConfigMap containing authorization configuration
Only used when Type is "configMap"

key.description: Key is the key in the ConfigMap that contains the authorization configuration

name.description: Name is the name of the ConfigMap

inline.description:
Inline contains direct authorization configuration
Only used when Type is "inline"

entitiesJson.description: EntitiesJSON is a JSON string representing Cedar entities

policies.description: Policies is a list of Cedar policy strings

type.description: Type is the type of authorization configuration

x-kubernetes-validations.message: configMap must be set when type is 'configMap', and must not be set otherwise
inline must be set when type is 'inline', and must not be set otherwise

backendReplicas.description:
BackendReplicas is the desired number of MCP server backend pod replicas.
This controls the backend Deployment (the MCP server container itself),
independent of the proxy runner controlled by Replicas.
When nil, the operator does not set Deployment.Spec.Replicas, leaving replica
management to an HPA or other external controller.

endpointPrefix.description:
EndpointPrefix is the path prefix to prepend to SSE endpoint URLs.
This is used to handle path-based ingress routing scenarios where the ingress
strips a path prefix before forwarding to the backend.

env.description: Env are environment variables to set in the MCP server container

items.description: EnvVar represents an environment variable in a container

externalAuthConfigRef.description:
ExternalAuthConfigRef references a MCPExternalAuthConfig resource for external authentication.
The referenced MCPExternalAuthConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPServer.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPExternalAuthConfig resource

groupRef.description:
GroupRef references the MCPGroup this server belongs to.
The referenced MCPGroup must be in the same namespace.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPGroup resource in the same namespace

image.description: Image is the container image for the MCP server

mcpPort.description: MCPPort is the port that MCP server listens to

oidcConfigRef.description:
OIDCConfigRef references a shared MCPOIDCConfig resource for OIDC authentication.
The referenced MCPOIDCConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPServer.
Per-server overrides (audience, scopes) are specified here; shared provider config
lives in the MCPOIDCConfig resource.

audience.description:
Audience is the expected audience for token validation.
This MUST be unique per server to prevent token replay attacks.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPOIDCConfig resource

resourceUrl.description:
ResourceURL is the public URL for OAuth protected resource metadata (RFC 9728).
When the server is exposed via Ingress or gateway, set this to the external
URL that MCP clients connect to. If not specified, defaults to the internal
Kubernetes service URL.

scopes.description:
Scopes is the list of OAuth scopes to advertise in the well-known endpoint (RFC 9728).
If empty, defaults to ["openid"].

permissionProfile.description: PermissionProfile defines the permission profile to use

key.description:
Key is the key in the ConfigMap that contains the permission profile
Only used when Type is "configmap"

name.description:
Name is the name of the permission profile
If Type is "builtin", Name must be one of: "none", "network"
If Type is "configmap", Name is the name of the ConfigMap

type.description: Type is the type of permission profile reference

podTemplateSpec.description:
PodTemplateSpec defines the pod template to use for the MCP server
This allows for customizing the pod configuration beyond what is provided by the other fields.
Note that to modify the specific container the MCP server runs in, you must specify
the `mcp` container name in the PodTemplateSpec.
This field accepts a PodTemplateSpec object as JSON/YAML.

proxyMode.description:
ProxyMode is the proxy mode for stdio transport (sse or streamable-http)
This setting is ONLY applicable when Transport is "stdio".
For direct transports (sse, streamable-http), this field is ignored.
The default value is applied by Kubernetes but will be ignored for non-stdio transports.

proxyPort.description: ProxyPort is the port to expose the proxy runner on

rateLimiting.description:
RateLimiting defines rate limiting configuration for the MCP server.
Requires Redis session storage to be configured for distributed rate limiting.

perUser.description:
PerUser is a token bucket applied independently to each authenticated user
at the server level. Requires authentication to be enabled.
Each unique userID creates Redis keys that expire after 2x refillPeriod.
Memory formula: unique_users_per_TTL_window * (1 + num_tools_with_per_user_limits) keys.

maxTokens.description:
MaxTokens is the maximum number of tokens (bucket capacity).
This is also the burst size: the maximum number of requests that can be served
instantaneously before the bucket is depleted.

refillPeriod.description:
RefillPeriod is the duration to fully refill the bucket from zero to maxTokens.
The effective refill rate is maxTokens / refillPeriod tokens per second.
Format: Go duration string (e.g., "1m0s", "30s", "1h0m0s").

shared.description: Shared is a token bucket shared across all users for the entire server.

maxTokens.description:
MaxTokens is the maximum number of tokens (bucket capacity).
This is also the burst size: the maximum number of requests that can be served
instantaneously before the bucket is depleted.

refillPeriod.description:
RefillPeriod is the duration to fully refill the bucket from zero to maxTokens.
The effective refill rate is maxTokens / refillPeriod tokens per second.
Format: Go duration string (e.g., "1m0s", "30s", "1h0m0s").

tools.description:
Tools defines per-tool rate limit overrides.
Each entry applies additional rate limits to calls targeting a specific tool name.
A request must pass both the server-level limit and the per-tool limit.

items.description:
ToolRateLimitConfig defines rate limits for a specific tool.
At least one of shared or perUser must be configured.

name.description: Name is the MCP tool name this limit applies to.

perUser.description: PerUser token bucket configuration for this tool.

maxTokens.description:
MaxTokens is the maximum number of tokens (bucket capacity).
This is also the burst size: the maximum number of requests that can be served
instantaneously before the bucket is depleted.

refillPeriod.description:
RefillPeriod is the duration to fully refill the bucket from zero to maxTokens.
The effective refill rate is maxTokens / refillPeriod tokens per second.
Format: Go duration string (e.g., "1m0s", "30s", "1h0m0s").

shared.description: Shared token bucket for this specific tool.

maxTokens.description:
MaxTokens is the maximum number of tokens (bucket capacity).
This is also the burst size: the maximum number of requests that can be served
instantaneously before the bucket is depleted.

refillPeriod.description:
RefillPeriod is the duration to fully refill the bucket from zero to maxTokens.
The effective refill rate is maxTokens / refillPeriod tokens per second.
Format: Go duration string (e.g., "1m0s", "30s", "1h0m0s").

x-kubernetes-validations.message: at least one of shared or perUser must be configured
at least one of shared, perUser, or tools must be configured

replicas.description:
Replicas is the desired number of proxy runner (thv run) pod replicas.
MCPServer creates two separate Deployments: one for the proxy runner and one
for the MCP server backend. This field controls the proxy runner Deployment.
When nil, the operator does not set Deployment.Spec.Replicas, leaving replica
management to an HPA or other external controller.

resourceOverrides.description: ResourceOverrides allows overriding annotations and labels for resources created by the operator

proxyDeployment.description: ProxyDeployment defines overrides for the Proxy Deployment resource (toolhive proxy)

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

env.description:
Env are environment variables to set in the proxy container (thv run process)
These affect the toolhive proxy itself, not the MCP server it manages
Use TOOLHIVE_DEBUG=true to enable debug logging in the proxy

items.description: EnvVar represents an environment variable in a container

imagePullSecrets.description:
ImagePullSecrets allows specifying image pull secrets for the proxy runner
These are applied to both the Deployment and the ServiceAccount

items.description:
LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the
referenced object inside the same namespace.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

podTemplateMetadataOverrides.description: ResourceMetadataOverrides defines metadata overrides for a resource

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

proxyService.description: ProxyService defines overrides for the Proxy Service resource (points to the proxy deployment)

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

resources.description: Resources defines the resource requirements for the MCP server container

limits.description: Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed

cpu.description: CPU is the CPU limit in cores (e.g., "500m" for 0.5 cores)

memory.description: Memory is the memory limit in bytes (e.g., "64Mi" for 64 megabytes)

requests.description: Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required

cpu.description: CPU is the CPU limit in cores (e.g., "500m" for 0.5 cores)

memory.description: Memory is the memory limit in bytes (e.g., "64Mi" for 64 megabytes)

secrets.description: Secrets are references to secrets to mount in the MCP server container

items.description: SecretRef is a reference to a secret

key.description: Key is the key in the secret itself

name.description: Name is the name of the secret

targetEnvName.description:
TargetEnvName is the environment variable to be used when setting up the secret in the MCP server
If left unspecified, it defaults to the key

serviceAccount.description:
ServiceAccount is the name of an already existing service account to use by the MCP server.
If not specified, a ServiceAccount will be created automatically and used by the MCP server.

sessionAffinity.description:
SessionAffinity controls whether the Service routes repeated client connections to the same pod.
MCP protocols (SSE, streamable-http) are stateful, so ClientIP is the default.
Set to "None" for stateless servers or when using an external load balancer with its own affinity.

sessionStorage.description:
SessionStorage configures session storage for stateful horizontal scaling.
When nil, no session storage is configured.

address.description: Address is the Redis server address (required when provider is redis)

db.description: DB is the Redis database number

keyPrefix.description: KeyPrefix is an optional prefix for all Redis keys used by ToolHive

passwordRef.description: PasswordRef is a reference to a Secret key containing the Redis password

key.description: Key is the key within the secret

name.description: Name is the name of the secret

provider.description: Provider is the session storage backend type

telemetryConfigRef.description:
TelemetryConfigRef references an MCPTelemetryConfig resource for shared telemetry configuration.
The referenced MCPTelemetryConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPServer.
Cross-namespace references are not supported for security and isolation reasons.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPTelemetryConfig resource

serviceName.description:
ServiceName overrides the telemetry service name for this specific server.
This MUST be unique per server for proper observability (e.g., distinguishing
traces and metrics from different servers sharing the same collector).
If empty, defaults to the server name with "thv-" prefix at runtime.

toolConfigRef.description:
ToolConfigRef references a MCPToolConfig resource for tool filtering and renaming.
The referenced MCPToolConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPServer.
Cross-namespace references are not supported for security and isolation reasons.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPToolConfig resource in the same namespace

transport.description: Transport is the transport method for the MCP server (stdio, streamable-http or sse)

trustProxyHeaders.description:
TrustProxyHeaders indicates whether to trust X-Forwarded-* headers from reverse proxies
When enabled, the proxy will use X-Forwarded-Proto, X-Forwarded-Host, X-Forwarded-Port,
and X-Forwarded-Prefix headers to construct endpoint URLs

volumes.description: Volumes are volumes to mount in the MCP server container

items.description: Volume represents a volume to mount in a container

hostPath.description: HostPath is the path on the host to mount

mountPath.description: MountPath is the path in the container to mount to

name.description: Name is the name of the volume

readOnly.description: ReadOnly specifies whether the volume should be mounted read-only

x-kubernetes-validations.message: per-tool perUser rate limiting requires authentication (oidcConfigRef or externalAuthConfigRef)

status.description: MCPServerStatus defines the observed state of MCPServer

authServerConfigHash.description:
AuthServerConfigHash is the hash of the referenced authServerRef spec,
used to detect configuration changes and trigger reconciliation.

conditions.description: Conditions represent the latest available observations of the MCPServer's state

items.description: Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This may be an empty string.

observedGeneration.description:
observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description:
reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.
Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,
and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.
The value should be a CamelCase string.
This field may not be empty.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description: type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.

externalAuthConfigHash.description: ExternalAuthConfigHash is the hash of the referenced MCPExternalAuthConfig spec

message.description: Message provides additional information about the current phase

observedGeneration.description: ObservedGeneration reflects the generation most recently observed by the controller

oidcConfigHash.description: OIDCConfigHash is the hash of the referenced MCPOIDCConfig spec for change detection

phase.description: Phase is the current phase of the MCPServer

readyReplicas.description: ReadyReplicas is the number of ready proxy replicas

telemetryConfigHash.description: TelemetryConfigHash is the hash of the referenced MCPTelemetryConfig spec for change detection

toolConfigHash.description: ToolConfigHash stores the hash of the referenced ToolConfig for change detection

url.description: URL is the URL where the MCP server can be accessed
